'''Luke Yates''' (born 6 March 1995) is an Australian professional [[rugby league]] footballer who plays as a {{rlp|lf}} and {{rlp|pr}} for the [[Warrington Wolves]] in the [[Super League]].

He previously played for the [[Newcastle Knights]] in the [[National Rugby League|NRL]], and the [[Huddersfield Giants]], the [[London Broncos]] and the [[Salford Red Devils]] in the [[Super League]].

==Background==
Yates was born in [[Newcastle, New South Wales]], [[Australia]].

He played his junior rugby league for the [[Western Suburbs Rosellas]], before being signed by the [[Newcastle Knights]].

==Playing career==
===Early career===
From 2013 to 2015, Yates played for the Newcastle Knights' [[National Youth Competition (rugby league)|NYC]] team.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://nycdatabase.org/players/y/|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20170301010840/https://nycdatabase.org/players/y/|url-status=usurped|archive-date=1 March 2017|title=Y|publisher=Nyc Database|date=|accessdate=2017-02-28}}</ref> In May 2015, he re-signed with the Newcastle club on two-year contract until the end of 2017.<ref>{{cite web|author=NRL Digital Media|url=http://www.newcastleknights.com.au/news/2015/05/25/luke_yates_re_signs.html|title=Luke Yates re-signs - Knights|publisher=Newcastleknights.com.au|date=|accessdate=2017-02-28}}</ref>

In July 2015, he played for the [[New South Wales rugby league team|New South Wales]] under-20s team against the [[Queensland rugby league team|Queensland]] under-20s team.<ref>{{cite web|author=|url=http://www.nswrl.com.au/news/2015/07/04/pay_s_say_nsw_under_.html|title=PAY'S SAY: NSW Under-20s side|publisher=NSWRL.com.au|date=|accessdate=2015-07-09}}</ref>

In 2016, he graduated to Newcastle's [[Intrust Super Premiership NSW]] team.<ref>{{cite web|author=New South Wales Rugby League|url=http://leagueunlimited.com/news/leagueunlimited-nsw-isp-teams-2016-round-7/|title=LeagueUnlimited Intrust Super Premiership NSW Teams - 2016 Round 7|publisher=League Unlimited|date=2016-04-12|accessdate=2017-02-28}}</ref>

===Newcastle Knights===
In round 1 of the [[2017 NRL season]], Yates made his [[National Rugby League|NRL]] debut for Newcastle against the [[New Zealand Warriors]].<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.nrl.com/updated-team-lists-warriors-v-knights/tabid/10874/newsid/104285/default.aspx |title=Updated team lists: Warriors v Knights |publisher=NRL.com |date=2016-12-18 |accessdate=2017-03-05}}</ref><ref>{{cite web |author=NRL Digital Media |url=http://www.newcastleknights.com.au/news/2017/03/05/late_mail_warriors_v.af_news.html |title=LATE MAIL: Warriors v Knights - Knights |publisher=Newcastleknights.com.au |date= |accessdate=2017-03-05 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20170305192119/http://www.newcastleknights.com.au/news/2017/03/05/late_mail_warriors_v.af_news.html |archive-date=5 March 2017 |url-status=dead }}</ref>

Yates made a total of 19 appearances for Newcastle in his debut year as the club finished with the wooden spoon for a third consecutive year.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.nrl.com/news/2017/12/12/knights-2017-by-the-numbers/|title=Knights: 2017 by the numbers|website=NRL}}</ref>

In September, he re-signed with Newcastle on a one- contract until the end of 2018.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.zerotackle.com/luke-yates-re-signs-knights-29414/|title=Luke Yates re-signs with Knights - Zero Tackle|date=27 September 2017|publisher=|accessdate=27 September 2017}}</ref> He finished the year with 19 appearances.<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.rugbyleagueproject.org/matches/Custom/MTUtMTY5Ni00LS0tLS0tMjM0MDQtLS0tLS0tLS0tLS0t|title=Custom Match List - Rugby League Project|first=Shawn Dollin and Andrew|last=Ferguson|website=www.rugbyleagueproject.org|accessdate=20 October 2018}}</ref>
[[File:Luke Yates.jpg|thumb|right|Yates playing for the Newcastle Knights in 2018]]
Yates only managed to play 6 NRL matches for the Newcastle club in 2018 before parting ways at the end of the season.<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.rugbyleagueproject.org/matches/Custom/MTUtMTcxNy00LS0tLS0tMjM0MDQtLS0tLS0tLS0tLS0t|title=Custom Match List - Rugby League Project|first=Shawn Dollin and Andrew|last=Ferguson|website=www.rugbyleagueproject.org|accessdate=20 October 2018}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.newcastleherald.com.au/story/5697285/knights-close-the-door-on-tough-tackling-local-junior-luke-yates/|title=Knights close the door on tough-tackling local junior Luke Yates|first=BARRY|last=TOOHEY|date=11 October 2018|publisher=|access-date=20 October 2018}}</ref>

===London Broncos===
Ahead of the 2019 season, Yates joined the [[London Broncos]] in the [[Super League]] on a one-year contract.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.newcastleherald.com.au/story/5775517/column-tooheys-news-knights-reject-dragons-bid-for-player-swap/?cs=7580|title=COLUMN - Toohey's News - Knights reject Dragons bid for player swap|first=BARRY|last=TOOHEY|date=24 November 2018|publisher=|access-date=26 November 2018}}</ref>

He was second in the Top Tackler award at the Betfred Super League Awards evening on 6 October 2019, after completing 1211 tackles over the course of 28 games in the [[2019 Super League season]].{{citation needed|date=October 2022}}

After being relegated from the Super League on points difference, he left the club at the end of his contract.

===Salford Red Devils===
Yates signed for [[Salford Red Devils|Salford]] from the London Broncos in October 2019. During the 2020 season he signed a two-year contract extension, which would see him through to the end of the 2022 season.

On 17 October 2020, Yates played in the [[2020 Challenge Cup Final]] defeat for Salford against Leeds at [[Wembley Stadium]].<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/54555957|title=Leeds beat Salford 17-16 after Luke Gale lands late winning drop-goal|website=www.bbc.co.uk}}</ref>

===Huddersfield Giants===
On 24 December 2020, it was announced that Yates would join the [[Huddersfield Giants]] from the 2021 season.<ref>{{cite web|website=BBC Sport|date=24 Dec 2020|accessdate=24 Dec 2020|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/55435947|title=Huddersfield Giants: Josh Jones and Luke Yates join Super League club}}</ref> He joined Huddersfield for a significant transfer fee as his Salford contract still had two seasons left to run. He was made team captain for Huddersfield for the 2022 season
[[File:Yateshg.jpg|thumb|Luke Yates at Huddersfield in 2021]]

On 28 May 2022, Yates played for Huddersfield in their [[2022 Challenge Cup Final]] loss to [[Wigan Warriors|Wigan]].<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.examinerlive.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/challenge-cup-player-ratings-final-24089809/|title=Challenge Cup player ratings as numerous stars shine in enthralling contest|website=www.examinerlive.co.uk}}</ref>
Yates played 24 matches for Huddersfield in the 2023 Super League season as the club finished ninth on the table and missed the playoffs.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.totalrl.com/ian-watson-explains-why-huddersfield-giants-underperformed-in-super-league-2023/#:~:text=HUDDERSFIELD%20GIANTS%20have%20been%20one,silverware%20once%20more%20this%20season./|title=Ian Watson explains why Huddersfield Giants underperformed in Super League 2023|website=www.totalrl.com}}</ref>
Yates played 14 games for Huddersfield in the 2024 Super League season which saw the club finish 9th on the table.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.yorkshirepost.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/huddersfield-giants-34-castleford-tigers-10-hosts-win-dead-rubber-but-2024-season-one-to-forget-for-west-yorkshire-rivals-4789784/|title=Huddersfield Giants 34 Castleford Tigers 10: Hosts win dead rubber but 2024 season one to forget for West Yorkshire rivals|website=www.yorkshirepost.co.uk}}</ref>

===Warrington Wolves===
On 15 July 2024 it was reported that he had signed for [[Warrington Wolves]] in the [[Super League]] initially on loan until the end of the 2024 season, and then a permanent two-year deal thereafter.<ref>{{cite web|website=Warrington Wolves|date=15 Jul 2024|title=Warrington Wolves is delighted to confirm that Luke Yates has joined with immediate effect on loan from Huddersfield Giants|url=https://warringtonwolves.com/news/2024/july/Yates-joins-with-immediate-effect/}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|website=Love Rugby League|date=15 Jul 2024|author=Aaron Bower|title=Warrington Wolves pay transfer fee to bring Huddersfield Giants forward to club early|url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/warrington-wolves-pay-transfer-fee-to-bring-huddersfield-giants-forward-to-club-early}}</ref>
On 7 June 2025, Yates played in Warrington's 8-6 Challenge Cup final loss against Hull Kingston Rovers.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/news/articles/cn0gnqllpxlo|title=In pictures: Hull KR Challenge Cup victory parade|website=www.bbc.co.uk}}</ref>

===Representative===
On [[International rugby league in 2021#June|25 June 2021]] he played for the [[Combined Nationalities rugby league team|Combined Nations All Stars]] in their 26-24 victory over [[England national rugby league team|England]], staged at the [[Halliwell Jones Stadium]], [[Warrington]], as part of England’s [[2021 Rugby League World Cup]] preparation.{{citation needed|date=October 2022}}
